title: triticum turgidum subsp. dicoccoides
intro: Triticum turgidum subsp. dicoccoides, also known as wild emmer or dicoccon, is a subspecies of wheat that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which help to alleviate various health issues. The health benefits of Triticum turgidum subsp. dicoccoides include its ability to regulate blood sugar levels, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
